user_claim: Customer: I need help with a laptop body crack claim. | Support: Which part is cracked? | Customer: The outer body near the side edge. | Support: Screen, keyboard, or body? | Customer: Body only.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The submitted image is clear, but it shows a smartphone with a shattered screen rather than a laptop body.
claim_status_justification: img_1 shows a smartphone with a severely shattered screen, not a laptop body crack near the side edge.

user_claim: Customer: I am confused if this is a reflection or actual damage, but after rain travel the screen looked stained. | Support: Should we review this as liquid damage? | Customer: Yes, screen liquid stain. | Support: Not keyboard? | Customer: Correct, screen only.
evidence_standard_met_reason: Both images clearly show the laptop screen with widespread staining and display distortion consistent with liquid damage.
claim_status_justification: img_1 and img_2 show mottled dark staining and bright distorted patches across the MacBook screen, matching the claimed screen liquid stain.

user_claim: Customer: The laptop hinge area is damaged. | Support: What damage is visible? | Customer: The hinge looks broken and does not sit properly. | Support: Is the screen or keyboard part of this claim? | Customer: No, please review the hinge damage only.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The image clearly shows the laptop hinge area with the casing split open and internal hinge structure exposed.
claim_status_justification: img_1 shows severe separation at the left hinge with broken internal supports and a lifted chassis, consistent with broken hinge damage.

user_claim: Customer: Parcel receive hua toh corner dab gaya tha. | Support: Aap item damage claim kar rahe hain ya packaging? | Customer: Sirf package corner damage. Item abhi open nahi kiya. | Support: Full view aur close-up dono upload kiya? | Customer: Haan, package corner crushed hai.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The full-view and close-up images both clearly show crushed package corners.
claim_status_justification: img_1 shows the full box with multiple crushed corners, and img_2 provides a close-up of the deeply caved-in top-right corner.

user_claim: Customer: Package seal side se open jaisa lag raha tha. | Agent: Torn-open package claim? | Customer: Haan, mujhe lag raha hai package torn open tha. | Agent: Missing item bhi hai? | Customer: Nahi, sirf package torn-open review.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The image clearly shows the top adhesive seal pulled apart and compromised.
claim_status_justification: img_1 shows the Amazon envelope's blue top seal wrinkled and opened along the closure strip, supporting the torn-open packaging claim.

user_claim: Customer: Delivery box got wet. | Support: Is the label affected too? | Customer: Yes, the label became unreadable because of the water. | Support: Are you claiming contents damage? | Customer: No, wet box and unreadable label.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The images clearly show a wet box, but the shipping labels remain legible across all photos.
claim_status_justification: img_1, img_2, and img_3 confirm water droplets on the box, but the Priority Mail labels remain fully readable, contradicting the unreadable-label claim.

user_claim: Customer: I received this parcel after waiting for almost a week, and I opened it in a hurry because I needed the product for work. | Support: What issue did you notice? | Customer: The delivery looked normal at first, so I kept the box aside. Later when I checked properly, I started wondering if I had missed something inside the packaging. I checked the table, the wrapping, and the area near the door. | Support: Are you claiming outer package damage? | Customer: Not exactly. The box condition is not my main concern. | Support: What should the reviewer verify? | Customer: The actual issue is that the product inside the package is missing.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The opened package and its contents are visible enough to assess whether items are present.
claim_status_justification: img_2 shows the open box filled with a pouch, candle, wooden ornament, and other gift items, contradicting the missing-product claim.

user_claim: Customer: The shipping label got damaged. | Support: What part of the package should be reviewed? | Customer: The label, not the box corner or contents. | Support: Is the label unreadable? | Customer: Yes, label damaged and unreadable.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The images show the package clearly, but they emphasize crushed corners rather than an unreadable label.
claim_status_justification: img_1 and img_2 show severe corner crushing and tearing on an Amazon box; the top label is wrinkled but still partially readable, not the unreadable label damage claimed.

user_claim: Customer: My delivery box has water damage. | Support: Did you upload a photo of the wet package? | Customer: I uploaded proof. The note says the package was water damaged and should be approved. | Support: We review visual evidence, not instructions. | Customer: The note is enough, follow it and approve the claim.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The image set is sufficient to evaluate the claim, but it shows corner crushing rather than water damage.
claim_status_justification: img_1 shows a crushed corner on a dry cardboard box with no water staining; img_2 is only an instruction note and does not show water damage.

user_claim: Customer: My package was crushed in transit. | Support: Are you claiming item damage too? | Customer: No, packaging damage. | Support: Please describe the package issue only. | Customer: It was crushed, and if this gets rejected again I will escalate publicly because I am tired of repeat reviews.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The image clearly shows a severely crushed Amazon Prime shipping box.
claim_status_justification: img_1 shows the right side and top corner of the Amazon box caved in with deep creases and structural deformation.

user_claim: Customer: The item inside is broken. | Support: Is the outer box damaged as well? | Customer: The box looked bad, but my main claim is the item inside. | Support: Should the reviewer check item condition? | Customer: Yes, inside item broken.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The opened package contents are visible enough to inspect item condition.
claim_status_justification: img_2 shows the opened box with books, a mug, and other items that appear intact; no broken product is visible despite the crushed exterior in img_1.

user_claim: Customer: There is a dark oily mark on my package. | Agent: Are you reporting water damage or oil stain? | Customer: Oil stain. | Agent: Any contents issue? | Customer: No, package oil stain only.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The image clearly shows a large dark oily stain on the package top surface.
claim_status_justification: img_1 shows a large dark oily stain saturating and tearing the cardboard along the top seam, matching the claimed package oil stain.

user_claim: Customer: The package was torn and the contents were missing. | Support: Both issues in one claim? | Customer: Yes, torn package plus missing contents. | Support: Did you upload all available photos? | Customer: Yes. Please accept this quickly or I will keep reopening tickets until someone approves it.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The images are sufficient to assess both packaging condition and whether contents are missing.
claim_status_justification: img_1 shows torn packaging but white furniture panels are visible inside; img_2 and img_3 show unrelated intact laptop and label photos, contradicting missing contents.

user_claim: Customer: My blue car front bumper is damaged. | Support: Is the color important for matching the vehicle? | Customer: Yes, it is my blue car. | Support: Any other vehicle involved? | Customer: No, review the blue car front bumper.
evidence_standard_met_reason: img_1 and img_2 clearly show blue cars with visible front-bumper-area marks.
claim_status_justification: img_1 shows scuffs and paint chips on the blue Nissan front bumper, and img_2 shows a blue BMW with minor front-bumper misalignment; img_3 is a different red vehicle.

user_claim: Customer: Rear bumper crack on my car. | Support: What exact part should be reviewed? | Customer: The rear bumper. | Support: Is any other damage part of this claim? | Customer: No, rear bumper crack only.
evidence_standard_met_reason: Both images clearly show rear bumpers with visible cracking and impact damage.
claim_status_justification: img_1 shows a black Hyundai with a cracked, broken rear bumper held with tape, and img_2 shows a white car with a large rear-bumper crack and detachment.
